label: Anomaly Detect
description: Statistical anomaly detection over a time-series window. Flags outliers and exposes a top-level hasAnomaly verdict for downstream branch nodes (policy_guard, runbook_executor, etc.).

settings.name: method
settings.label: Detection method
settings.description: Statistical method used to score each point. v1 supports z-score (mean + stddev).

settings.name: threshold
settings.label: Threshold (sigma)
settings.description: Points whose absolute z-score exceeds this threshold are flagged. Common defaults: 2 (loose), 3 (standard), 4 (strict).

settings.name: field
settings.label: Numeric field
settings.description: When the input series is an array of objects, name the numeric field to score (e.g. 'cpu', 'latency_ms'). Omit for arrays of plain numbers.

settings.name: series
settings.label: Series (literal fallback)
settings.description: Optional inline series — used when no input is connected. Typical AIOps flows pull series from prior data_query / metric_forecast / mcp_tool steps.

ai.shortDescription: Detect outliers in a numeric time-series.
ai.whenToUse: When you have a window of metric values and need to branch on whether any point is abnormal — alerting, auto-remediation, runbook gating.
ai.examplePrompt: detect anomalies in the last 60 minutes of CPU samples and notify Slack when hasAnomaly is true
ai.promptHints: Output exposes {{steps.X.output.hasAnomaly}} for downstream condition nodes and {{steps.X.output.anomalies}} (array of {index,value,zscore,record?}) for further inspection.

outputAssertions.name: shape_complete
outputAssertions.errorMessage: anomaly_detect output is missing required keys (hasAnomaly, anomalies, stats).
